audit_policy.cost_note: Audit students pay the same tuition and fees as credit students. Senior citizens age 65+ may audit for free (tuition waived; local fees may apply).

senior_discount.cost: Free (up to 6 credit hours per semester)
senior_discount.notes: NC residents 65+ may audit up to 6 credit hours per semester for free under NC General Statute § 115D-5(b)(2), space permitting.

steps: Complete an admissions application if not already enrolled as a student
Register for the course through the normal registration process
Complete an Audit Request Form and obtain instructor approval
Submit the signed Audit Request Form to the Registrar or Records office before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)
application_process.timing: Audit requests must be submitted before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)

restrictions: Instructor approval may be required
Audit students receive a grade of AU — no credit or grade points awarded
Financial aid and veterans' benefits do not cover audited courses
A student auditing a course shall not displace a student enrolling for credit
Changing from audit to credit (or vice versa) must be done before the census date
All course prerequisites must be met

audit_policy.cost_note: Auditing requires payment of standard tuition and fees, same as credit enrollment. Senior citizens 65+ have tuition waived.

senior_discount.cost: Free (up to 6 credit hours per semester)
senior_discount.notes: NC residents 65+ may audit up to 6 credit hours per semester for free under NC General Statute § 115D-5(b)(2), space permitting.

steps: Register for the course through standard registration procedures
Submit a completed Request for Audit form to the Records and Registration Office within the first 15 days of the term
Obtain instructor approval (instructor must sign the form)
application_process.timing: Audit requests must be submitted before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)

restrictions: Must be enrolled at A-B Tech as a curriculum student to audit
Physical Education and Health/Fitness Science courses cannot be audited
Audit students receive AU grade — no credit toward diploma or degree
All prerequisites must be satisfied
Changing from credit to audit must be done within first 15 days of term
Changing from audit to credit must be done within first 5 days of term
Financial aid does not cover audited courses

audit_policy.cost_note: Audit students pay the same fees as regular enrollment. Senior citizens 65+ receive tuition waiver; local fees may apply.

senior_discount.cost: Free (up to 6 credit hours per semester)
senior_discount.notes: NC residents 65+ may audit up to 6 credit hours per semester for free under NC General Statute § 115D-5(b)(2), space permitting.

steps: Follow regular admissions and registration procedures
Complete the audit form online, indicating which course(s) to audit
Have the audit form approved by the course instructor
Submit the approved form to the Registrar's Office on or before the course start date (no later than the 10% date)
application_process.timing: Audit requests must be submitted before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)

restrictions: Instructor approval required
Auditing students must meet all course prerequisites
Students receive a grade of AU or SR (Senior Audit) — no credit awarded
Auditing students are not required to participate in discussion or take tests
A student auditing shall not displace a student enrolling for credit
An auditing student may be administratively dropped by the 10% date if a credit-seeking student needs the seat
Audit courses may be repeated a maximum of two times unless an exception is made
Financial aid does not cover audited courses

steps: Complete an audit agreement form (available as PDF from the Registrar's Office)
Have the form signed by the course instructor
Submit the signed form to the Registrar's office at any Central Piedmont campus before the census date
application_process.timing: Audit requests must be submitted before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)

Financial aid does not cover audited courses

steps: Consult with your program director at least 10 working days prior to the first day of the term
Complete and submit an Audit Request Form (available from registration@durhamtech.edu)
Obtain necessary approvals as directed by the program director
Register for the course and pay tuition and applicable fees
application_process.timing: Audit requests must be submitted before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)

restrictions: Must consult program director at least 10 working days before term starts
Audit students receive AU grade — no credit, CEU, or certificate of completion
An audit student may not supplant a student taking the course for credit
Subject to standard attendance requirements; may be required to complete coursework
Self-supporting courses are not eligible for auditing
Financial aid and veterans' benefits do not cover audited courses

Audit students receive a grade of AU — no credit or grade points awarded
Financial aid and veterans' benefits do not cover audited courses
A student auditing a course shall not displace a student enrolling for credit
Changing from audit to credit (or vice versa) must be done before the census date
All course prerequisites must be met

Submit the signed Audit Request Form to the Registrar or Records office before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)
application_process.timing: Audit requests must be submitted before the census date (typically the 10% point of the course)

restrictions: Instructor approval may be required
Audit students receive a grade of AU — no credit or grade points awarded
Financial aid and veterans' benefits do not cover audited courses
A student auditing a course shall not displace a student enrolling for credit
Changing from audit to credit (or vice versa) must be done before the census date
All course prerequisites must be met

audit_policy.cost_note: Audit students pay the same tuition and fees as credit students. Senior citizens age 65+ may audit for free (tuition waived; local fees may apply).

senior_discount.cost: Free (up to 6 credit hours per semester)
senior_discount.notes: NC residents 65+ may audit up to 6 credit hours per semester for free under NC General Statute § 115D-5(b)(2), space permitting.
